Arab armies invaded Egypt Babylon fell April 641, after siege some seven months.
Alexandria taken September 641, Egypt surrendered Byzantines November that year.
Arab commander, al-As, wanted establish capital city Alexandria, permission refused Caliph, Umar.
reason quoted refusal that want body water separate capital from Arabia, built east bank Nile; however, attempt keep control Egyptian forces their leader have been real motive.
capital city (called Fustat, possibly from Arabic tent, camp) thus established just north Babylon.
town laid series tribal areas, khittas, around central area with communal mosque, administrative buildings residences highest status tribal groups.
majority tribes settled here were Yemeni origin, with remainder Arabs from western Arabian peninsula; Jews some Roman mercenaries were also present.
this stage population must have consisted almost entirely soldiers their households, Fustat exhibited typical plan garrison town.
Originally founded Mosque still stands been continually rebuilt: nothing remains earliest structure.
Large piles stone- woodwork have been dumped across ruins town these almost certainly came from older versions Mosque include many reused Roman column capitals.
town grew rapidly size, expanding into desert onto higher ground.
became important commercial center, excavations site have found artifacts from places apart China, India, Vietnam, Iraq, Italy Spain.
Museum Islamic contains many objects from Fustat.
city retain status capital Egypt; other administrative districts were planned built north Fustat: Abbasid al-Askar, Tulunid al-Qata'i great Fatimid town al-Qahira, which gives modern Cairo name.
However, Fustat remained important trading post eastern Mediterranean world.
1168 town completely burned prevent Crusaders, recovered prosperity population fairlyquickly.
Fustat eventually declined importance trade-routes between East Europe shifted northwards, depriving town much income.
unlikely, though, that ever completely abandoned, until recently potters' quarter existed upon site.
Today, little remains rich city that once flourished site.
area behind Mosque become convenient place Cairo citizens dump their rubbish desolate unhygienic place.
Further east, around ticket office, house walls still stand streets traced.

27-year-old star coached four seasons Chicago, Thibodeau sent promising youngsters Zach LaVine Kris Dunn well Thursday's seventh pick Bulls.
Wolves also received back draft's 16th pick, which Bulls used select Creighton 6-11 freshman center Justin Patton Minnesota.
Bulls used Wolves' seventh pick acquire Arizona 7-foot shooter Lauri Markkanen.
year after teams discussed such trade, Thibodeau General Manager Scott Layden pulled their first together.

Sunday, March 2019, most U.S. switched Daylight time from Standard time.
U.S. makes change.
Arizona, except Navajo Nation, Hawaii, American Samoa, Guam, Northern Mariana Islands, Puerto Rico U.S. Virgin Islands don't switch Daylight time spring back Standard time fall.
Their clocks always Standard time.
travel internationally, it's important aware that Daylight/Standard time change doesn't universally occur across globe.
More than countries never change Daylight time more than countries switch between different dates than other countries.
Lithium based batteries commercial passenger aircraft have been regulated since 2008.
That year, U.S. Department Transportation DOT) banned spare lithium based batteries from passengers' checked luggage.
last month, formalized what U.S. based commercial passenger carriers already been doing.
They banned "transport lithium-ion cells batteries cargo passenger aircraft."
ICAO (International Civil Aviation Organization) called since February, 2016.
deadly health effects tobacco were made known 1964, when Luther Terry, M.D., then Surgeon General U.S., released first report Surgeon General's Advisory Committee Smoking Health.
From that report subsequent ones, know that smokers more likely than non-smokers develop heart disease, stroke, lung cancer myriad other serious diseases.
According CDC, statistics about smokers contracting life threatening diseases compared non-smokers startling.
Smokers more likely contract coronary heart disease 200%-400%.
Smokers more likely experience stroke 200%-400%.
Smokers more likely from Chronic Obstructive Pulmonary Disease (COPD) 1,200%-1,300%.
Cigarette smoke extremely toxic.
contains about 4,000 active compounds, including tar, polycyclic aromatic hydrocarbons, formaldehyde, carbon monoxide, heavy metals.
